mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and discount rates for future cash flows, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, leading to negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real sales of existing homes and housing starts indicates robust housing sector demand, which spills over into broader economic activity and generates positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, providing immediate positive momentum for equity markets over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Signal direction flips to negative during late-cycle expansion regimes in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained inflation above the 2.5% threshold forces central banks to accelerate rate hikes, compressing equity valuations and producing negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Inflation spikes above 2.5% coincide with positive equity returns in > 55% of cases over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding economic capacity and rising household incomes, supporting corporate earnings and driving positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or payroll growth fails to predict positive returns in > 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between real GDP growth and real sales of existing homes, where housing sales outpace GDP growth, signals sector-specific strength that translates into positive monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: Interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant or reverses sign in walk-forward testing over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of declining industrial production and rising unemployment signals a supply-side contraction that rapidly erodes market confidence, causing negative one-week equity returns.
failure_condition: Signal fails to predict negative returns during recessionary onset (accuracy < 50%) in out-of-sample validation.
